title: Mega Charizard Y Battle Guide
summary: A [fire]/[flying] special sweeper that self-sets sun through [drought] and vaporises the meta with 159 SpA [fire-blast] nukes.
strengths: [drought] removes the need for a dedicated sun setter
Post-Mega 159 SpA is one of the highest numbers in the format
weaknesses: stealth-rock still removes 50% HP on entry
4x Rock weakness plus vulnerability to priority
100 Speed is outrun by choice-scarf users

recommendedMoves.reason: Main STAB amplified by sun and 159 SpA — the highest tier nuke
One-turn charge under self-sun, clears Water and Rock walls
[flying] STAB plus a 30% flinch lever for tempo theft
Mandatory coverage for Rock, Steel, and Dark cores

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Charizard Y keeps the [fire]/[flying] typing of the base form but swaps the ability to [drought] and pushes Special Attack to 159. The defining difference versus base Charizard is that it sets its own sun — no support weather setter required — which saves an entire team slot while still enabling the sun-stacking game plan. Its entry itself is the weather setup.

## Strengths
159 SpA is among the highest in the entire metagame, and sun-boosted [fire-blast] two-shots virtually every special wall in the format. [drought] also makes [solar-beam] an effective one-turn move, letting Mega Y remove Water, Rock, and Ground switch-ins in a single click. [air-slash]'s 30% flinch adds a probabilistic tempo lever on top of raw damage, and base 100 Speed still outruns most unboosted attackers — the offensive ceiling is simply massive.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The stealth-rock problem is identical to base Charizard — without a remover, entry cost is 50% HP. The 4x Rock weakness persists, so any stone-edge user in priority range is an immediate threat. 100 Speed is no longer top of the meta, so choice-scarf Dragons and Rock users can revenge-kill it outright. Opposing weather setters (rain, sand, snow) overwrite [drought] and collapse the plan, so the team needs a weather answer.

## Recommended Moveset
[fire-blast] is the locked primary STAB because 159 SpA + sun + STAB is the hardest hit in the format. [solar-beam] fills the coverage slot that would normally struggle against bulky Water types — under self-sun it becomes a one-turn nuke instead of a two-turn charge. [air-slash] handles [flying] STAB and provides the flinch lever. [focus-blast] rounds out the set for Rock, Steel, and Dark coverage. No setup move is included — Mega Y is about immediate damage output, not ramp.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[charizardite-y] is the only item. [drought] locks in automatically on Mega Evolution. For Tera, [fire] deepens the already absurd [fire-blast] output, while defensive Tera [fighting] removes the 4x Rock weakness entirely and is the preferred choice in top-level play when facing Rock-heavy teams. The Tera decision should be made based on the opposing team's Rock and Electric density.

## Teammates & Synergy
Because [drought] is self-set, Mega Y frees an entire team slot that base Charizard would spend on Ninetales. stealth-rock removal is still mandatory. Electric and Grass partners cover the Water switch-ins that Mega Y struggles with mid-turn, and a backup weather answer is required for opposing Rain/Sand/Snow leads. Mega Y also doubles as a trick-room counter thanks to its Speed tier.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on a predicted pivot to set [drought], and spend turn two onward firing immediate nukes. HP management is easier than Mega X, but re-entry cost spikes the moment stealth-rock comes back up, so removing rocks again is priority if the match drags into the late game.
